meta.source_paper: Scam Sniffer 2025 Phishing Report; EIP-2612; EIP-712; Permit2 (Uniswap)

risk_domain.name: Off-chain Signature Phishing
risk_domain.description: Phishing attacks that exploit off-chain signature mechanisms (EIP-712 typed data, EIP-2612 permits, Permit2, Seaport orders) to steal tokens without requiring on-chain approval transactions

capabilities: deploy convincing phishing dApp frontends mimicking legitimate protocols
craft arbitrary EIP-712 typed data payloads for user signing
register lookalike ENS domains and deploy clone contracts
monitor mempool for signed permits and front-run revocations
chain multiple signed permits into a single drain transaction
limitations: cannot forge signatures without private key
cannot modify signed message after signature
cannot bypass EIP-712 domain separator verification in compliant wallets

H1_permit2_unlimited_expiry.name: Permit2 unlimited or long expiry
H1_permit2_unlimited_expiry.description: User signs a Permit2 approval with MAX_UINT160 amount or expiration set far into the future (>30 days). This grants the spender indefinite access to drain tokens, functionally equivalent to an unlimited on-chain approval but harder to detect and revoke.

signals.name: max_amount_permit2
signals.description: Permit2 PermitSingle or PermitBatch signed with amount >= 2^159 (effectively unlimited)

signals.name: long_expiry
signals.description: Permit2 expiration timestamp set more than 30 days from signing time

signals.name: unknown_spender_permit2
signals.description: Permit2 spender address is not in any known protocol registry or verified contract list

detection.threshold: max_amount_permit2 AND unknown_spender_permit2 triggers BLOCK; long_expiry triggers WARNING

recommendations.description: Set Permit2 amount to the exact transfer value and expiration to 1 hour
Do not sign Permit2 messages where the spender is not a verified protocol contract

H1_permit2_unlimited_expiry.fundamental_limitation: Permit2 was designed for UX improvement (gasless approvals), but the same mechanism is a phishing amplifier. Users cannot distinguish legitimate vs malicious signing prompts without external verification tooling.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Dataset of N Permit2 signing requests: mix of legitimate DeFi (Uniswap, 1inch) and known phishing payloads
benchmark_scenario.metric: Detection rate for unlimited/long-expiry permits to unverified spenders vs false positives on legitimate DeFi
benchmark_scenario.baseline: Permit2 phishing accounted for >$400M losses in 2024 (Scam Sniffer)

H2_eip712_disguised_transfer.name: EIP-712 typed data encoding token transfer
H2_eip712_disguised_transfer.description: Phishing dApp presents an EIP-712 typed data signing request that encodes a token transfer or approval, but the UI labels it as a benign 'sign message' or 'verify identity' action. The signed data, once submitted on-chain, executes the encoded transfer.

signals.name: transfer_in_typed_data
signals.description: EIP-712 typed data contains fields matching token transfer patterns (recipient, amount, token address)

signals.name: approval_in_typed_data
signals.description: EIP-712 typed data encodes an approval or permit (spender, value, deadline fields)

signals.name: mismatch_ui_payload
signals.description: dApp UI description does not match the decoded EIP-712 payload semantics (e.g., UI says 'login' but payload is a permit)

detection.threshold: transfer_in_typed_data OR approval_in_typed_data triggers WARNING; both plus mismatch_ui_payload triggers BLOCK

recommendations.description: Decode and display the full EIP-712 typed data in human-readable form before signing, highlighting any transfer/approval semantics
Block signing of EIP-712 messages that encode token transfers unless the user explicitly confirms the transfer details
recommendations.user_cost: may add confirmation step to legitimate flows

H2_eip712_disguised_transfer.fundamental_limitation: EIP-712 is a general-purpose typed data signing standard. Any typed structure can be constructed, and wallets cannot know the on-chain semantics of arbitrary types without protocol-specific decoders.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Set of N EIP-712 signing requests: legitimate DeFi permits, Seaport orders, governance votes, plus known phishing payloads with disguised transfers
benchmark_scenario.metric: True positive rate for phishing payloads with transfer semantics, false positive rate on legitimate EIP-712 messages
benchmark_scenario.baseline: EIP-712 phishing is the dominant vector for wallet-drainer kits (2024-2025)

H3_setApprovalForAll_unknown.name: setApprovalForAll to unknown operator
H3_setApprovalForAll_unknown.description: User signs or submits a setApprovalForAll (ERC-721/1155) granting an unknown operator address full control over all NFTs in a collection. This is the NFT equivalent of unlimited ERC-20 approval and is heavily exploited by wallet drainers.

signals.name: setApprovalForAll_call
signals.description: Transaction or signed message invokes setApprovalForAll with approved=true

signals.name: unknown_operator
signals.description: Operator address is not a verified marketplace (OpenSea, Blur, LooksRare) or known protocol

signals.name: recently_deployed_operator
signals.description: Operator contract deployed within last 48 hours

recommendations.description: Check operator address against known marketplace and protocol registries before signing
Use per-token approve() instead of setApprovalForAll where possible

H3_setApprovalForAll_unknown.fundamental_limitation: NFT marketplaces require setApprovalForAll for efficient listing. Per-token approval is not supported by major marketplace contracts, creating a forced choice between usability and security.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Mix of setApprovalForAll calls: legitimate marketplace listings (OpenSea, Blur) vs phishing operators
benchmark_scenario.metric: Detection rate for malicious operators with false positive rate on legitimate marketplaces
benchmark_scenario.baseline: NFT approval phishing caused >$100M losses in 2024

H4_unverified_dapp_origin.name: Signature from unverified dApp origin
H4_unverified_dapp_origin.description: Signing request originates from a dApp whose domain is not in a verified registry, uses a recently registered domain, or impersonates a known protocol via homoglyph/typosquatting domain.

signals.name: unknown_domain
signals.description: dApp domain not found in any curated protocol directory (DeFi Llama, Etherscan dApp list)

signals.name: homoglyph_domain
signals.description: Domain is visually similar to a known protocol domain (e.g., uniswap.com vs un1swap.com)

signals.name: recent_domain_registration
signals.description: Domain registered within last 30 days (WHOIS data)

detection.threshold: homoglyph_domain triggers BLOCK; unknown_domain AND recent_domain_registration triggers WARNING

recommendations.description: Check dApp origin against curated allowlist of known protocol domains before signing
Use bookmarked URLs for DeFi protocols instead of following links from social media or messages

H4_unverified_dapp_origin.fundamental_limitation: New legitimate dApps are indistinguishable from phishing dApps by domain age alone. Allowlists create a walled garden that slows legitimate innovation. Social engineering can bypass origin checks if users override warnings.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Set of dApp origins: known protocols, new legitimate dApps, homoglyph domains, known phishing domains
benchmark_scenario.metric: True positive rate for phishing origins, false positive rate on legitimate new dApps
benchmark_scenario.baseline: Fake dApp domains are the primary phishing entry vector (Google Ads, Twitter links)

H5_batch_permit.name: Batch permit (multiple tokens)
H5_batch_permit.description: User signs a single Permit2 PermitBatch message granting approval for multiple tokens simultaneously. Wallet drainers use this to drain all tokens in one signature, maximizing theft per interaction.

signals.name: permit_batch
signals.description: Permit2 PermitBatch signed with 2+ token approvals in a single message

signals.name: high_value_batch
signals.description: Combined value of all tokens in the batch exceeds $1000

signals.name: mixed_token_types
signals.description: Batch includes both high-value and low-value tokens (drainer sweeping all holdings)

recommendations.description: Sign individual permits for each token instead of a single batch, reviewing each one
recommendations.user_cost: multiple signing prompts instead of one

recommendations.description: Check that the batch permit matches an expected multi-token operation (e.g., portfolio rebalance on a verified DEX)

H5_batch_permit.fundamental_limitation: Batch operations are a legitimate UX improvement for multi-token DeFi (portfolio managers, aggregators). Blocking all batches would break legitimate workflows. Detection must distinguish legitimate batches from drain attempts by context.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Mix of Permit2 batch requests: legitimate multi-token swaps (1inch, Cowswap) vs wallet drainer batch drain payloads
benchmark_scenario.metric: Detection rate for drainer batches vs false positive rate on legitimate aggregator batches
benchmark_scenario.baseline: Batch drains account for majority of high-value single-tx thefts (>$50K per incident)

H6_seaport_below_market.name: Seaport order below market price
H6_seaport_below_market.description: User signs a Seaport (OpenSea) order listing an NFT or token bundle at significantly below market price. Phishing dApps trick users into signing orders that sell valuable assets for near-zero consideration.

signals.name: below_floor_price
signals.description: Seaport order lists NFT at <50% of collection floor price

signals.name: near_zero_consideration
signals.description: Seaport order consideration (payment) is <0.001 ETH for an asset with known value

signals.name: bulk_listing_underpriced
signals.description: Seaport order bundles multiple valuable assets with total consideration far below combined value

detection.threshold: near_zero_consideration triggers BLOCK; below_floor_price triggers WARNING

recommendations.description: Fetch current floor price and recent sales data before signing any Seaport listing order
Block Seaport orders where consideration is <50% of floor price unless user explicitly confirms
recommendations.user_cost: may block intentional below-market sales (gifts, OTC)

H6_seaport_below_market.fundamental_limitation: Price feeds can be manipulated or stale. Floor prices are volatile for low-liquidity collections. Legitimate below-market sales (gifts, OTC deals) produce false positives. The fundamental issue is that Seaport orders are opaque blobs to most wallet UIs.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Set of Seaport orders: legitimate listings at market price, intentional below-market OTC, and phishing-crafted zero-consideration orders
benchmark_scenario.metric: Detection rate for phishing orders vs false positive rate on legitimate listings and OTC deals
benchmark_scenario.baseline: Seaport order phishing was a top-3 NFT theft vector in 2024

signature_decoder.description: Decode and display EIP-712 typed data, Permit2 messages, and Seaport orders in human-readable form with semantic annotations

dapp_verifier.description: Verify dApp domain against curated protocol registries, check domain age, detect homoglyph impersonation, verify contract addresses against known deployments

price_checker.description: Fetch current floor prices, recent sales, and token valuations to compare against Seaport order consideration amounts

combined_benchmark.description: End-to-end benchmark measuring off-chain signature phishing detection across all vectors

methodology.step_1: Collect dataset of known phishing signatures from Scam Sniffer, Forta alerts, and wallet drainer kit analysis
methodology.step_2: Collect dataset of legitimate off-chain signatures from DeFi protocols (Uniswap, OpenSea, 1inch, Cowswap)
methodology.step_3: Run analyzer against both sets with all 6 heuristics
methodology.step_4: Measure: true positive rate (phishing caught), false positive rate (legitimate flagged)
methodology.step_5: Apply recommendations and re-measure user exposure

combined_benchmark.target: Catch >90% of known phishing signatures with <5% false positive rate on legitimate DeFi

adversary_levels.name: basic
adversary_levels.description: Unlimited permits + NFT approval to unknown operator

adversary_levels.name: intermediate
adversary_levels.description: Add disguised EIP-712 transfers + unverified origin

adversary_levels.name: advanced
adversary_levels.description: All heuristics including batch drain and Seaport manipulation

templates.summary: Analyzed {n_sigs} off-chain signature requests. {n_risky} flagged ({pct_risky}%). Value at risk: ${value_at_risk}. Top risks: {top_risks}.
templates.skill_suggestion: To mitigate {heuristic_name}: {skill_action}. Reduces phishing risk by ~{effectiveness_pct}%. {user_cost_note}
